What is CEL, an English language school in San Diego, Los Angeles, and Vancouver?

CEL, the College of English Language, is an accredited English language school where international students aged 16 and older study across three campuses in North America: two in California (San Diego, and Santa Monica/Los Angeles) and one in downtown Vancouver, British Columbia. Class sizes average seven students per group (maximum twelve), the school holds a 4.8-star rating from more than 195 student reviews, and start dates open every Monday throughout the year. Programs run from one-week intensives through multi-month progressions toward advanced English fluency, and admissions support is available in nine languages including English, Spanish, German, French, Italian, Portuguese, Korean, Japanese, and Arabic.
Where you can learn English with CEL

Each campus has a distinct character that shapes the daily experience. CEL San Diego sits in the Pacific Beach neighborhood three blocks from the ocean, giving students year-round Southern California weather and a regular schedule of community events. CEL Los Angeles operates from the Santa Monica beachfront business district, putting students within walking distance of the entertainment, technology, and creative industries that define the region. CEL Vancouver is downtown near Coal Harbour, a Designated Learning Institution (DLI) in one of the world's most livable cities and the only Canadian destination in our network. The USA and Canada options each come with distinct visa pathways, accent exposure, and post-graduation work permit possibilities; our admissions team helps each student match nationality, timeline, and goals to the right campus.
How to choose your CEL program and CEFR level

The right program starts with the goal. Students building everyday fluency typically begin with General English, a level-tested course that runs from absolute beginner (Common European Framework of Reference level A1) through advanced (C1). Students preparing for university entry add specialty electives: Cambridge Preparation for B2 First and C1 Advanced certificates, Test of English as a Foreign Language internet-Based Test (TOEFL iBT) Preparation for North American university admission, Academic Skills for the writing and discussion abilities universities expect, or our Global Pathway program for students aiming to enter a US college directly. Career-focused students choose Career Development, which combines English fluency work with project-based learning in business communication, AI tools, and project management. The complete catalog lives on our courses page, and CEL admissions can match your starting level, timeline, and goal to the right combination of course and campus.
Pathways and outcomes after your English course at CEL

Many students extend their stay or move into our Pathway Program, which prepares international students for direct admission to American colleges and universities through partnerships covering certificate programs with Optional Practical Training (OPT) eligibility, undergraduate transfer agreements, and graduate program preparation. Others use their improved English to apply to universities directly, qualify for jobs that require English at multinationals in their home countries, or secure Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP) status in Canada, which allows eligible graduates of recognized programs to work in the country for up to three years with a documented route to permanent residency through Express Entry. We help arrange student housing at all three campuses, from homestays with local families through shared apartments and student residences. Most new students start with a free consultation with our admissions team, who can walk through visa pathways, course selection, accommodation, and timeline based on specific goals and country of origin.

What does it mean to learn English in the USA with CEL?

Learning English in the USA with CEL means studying at one of three California campuses run by a Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP) certified school that has welcomed international students aged 16 and older for decades. How visas and entry work for studying English in the USA

Most international students enter the USA on an F-1 student visa, which requires enrollment in an SEVP-certified program (CEL is certified) and proof of financial support for tuition and living costs. Students from Visa Waiver Program countries (most of Europe, plus Japan, Korea, Australia, and several others) studying for under 90 days can enter on an Electronic System for Travel Authorization (ESTA), which is sufficient for short courses and summer programs but not for full-length stays. For any course longer than 90 days, the F-1 visa is required. CEL admissions issues the I-20 form (the document a school must provide for F-1 applications), helps with US embassy interview preparation, and times the application to your course start date. The full F-1 process from CEL invitation to USA arrival typically takes 2 to 6 months depending on country and embassy availability.

Students looking for cultural immersion can add electives such as English Plus Experience (which pairs English study with a placement at a California-based company) or English Plus Surfing in San Diego.
